SEARCH
Package server.net

Class ClientSession

java.lang.Object
server.net.ClientSession
public class ClientSession extends Object
Per-connection server-side state for one client.

Wraps the live socket and its two object streams, and tracks the set of SubscriptionKeys the client is currently watching on the realtime push channel.

All outbound traffic on this connection — both replies to client requests (sendResponse(ServerResponse)) and unsolicited pushes from the subscription registry (sendEvent(ServerEvent)) — funnels through the single private writeLock. That shared lock is intentional: the request-handling thread and the publisher thread share one ObjectOutputStream, and interleaved writeObject calls would produce garbled, undeserializable bytes on the wire. Sequencing them with the same lock guarantees each frame ships atomically.

    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private final ObjectInputStream
    in
    Object input stream for reading requests.
    private Long
    loggedInActorId
    Id of the actor authenticated on this connection, or null if none.
    private String
    loggedInKind
    Single-login lock kind held ("USER"/"VISITOR"), or null.
    private final ObjectOutputStream
    out
    Object output stream for writing responses/events.
    private final Socket
    socket
    The client's socket.
    private final Set<SubscriptionKey>
    subscriptions
    The push subscriptions this connection currently holds.
    private final ReentrantLock
    writeLock
    Serializes outbound writes so frames ship atomically.

    ClientSession(Socket socket, ObjectInputStream in, ObjectOutputStream out)

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    Closes the streams and the socket.
    Long
    getLoggedInActorId()
    Returns the id of the actor authenticated on this connection, or null if no one is logged in.
    String
    getLoggedInKind()
    Returns the single-login lock kind held by this connection ("USER"/"VISITOR"), or null if logged out.
    String
    remoteAddressString()
    Returns string form of the peer's socket address (or "(unknown)" if the address is no longer available) for use in log lines.
    void
    sendEvent(ServerEvent ev)
    Pushes an unsolicited event from the server to this subscribed client.
    void

    Sends a reply to a client request, serializing access to the output stream so it never interleaves with a concurrent sendEvent(common.dto.ServerEvent).
    void
    setLoggedIn(Long actorId, String kind)
    Records (on login) or clears (on logout) who is authenticated on this connection and which single-login lock kind they hold.
    void

    Records that this client now wants change events for key.
    Set<SubscriptionKey>
    subscriptions()
    Read-only snapshot of every subscription this session currently holds.
    void
    unsubscribe(SubscriptionKey key)
    Removes a previous subscription.

  • Field Details

    • socket

      private final Socket socket
      The client's socket.

    • in

      private final ObjectInputStream in
      Object input stream for reading requests.

    • out

      private final ObjectOutputStream out
      Object output stream for writing responses/events.

    • subscriptions

      private final Set<SubscriptionKey> subscriptions
      The push subscriptions this connection currently holds.

    • writeLock

      private final ReentrantLock writeLock
      Serializes outbound writes so frames ship atomically.

    • loggedInActorId

      private Long loggedInActorId
      Id of the actor authenticated on this connection, or null if none.

    • loggedInKind

      private String loggedInKind
      Single-login lock kind held ("USER"/"VISITOR"), or null.

  • Constructor Details

    • ClientSession

      public ClientSession(Socket socket, ObjectInputStream in, ObjectOutputStream out)
      Wraps an already-connected socket and its already-constructed object streams.
      Parameters:
      socket - the connected client socket; owned by this session and closed by close()
      in - input stream for reading ClientRequests
      out - output stream for writing responses and pushed events

  • Method Details

    • sendResponse

      public void sendResponse(ServerResponse resp) throws IOException
      Sends a reply to a client request, serializing access to the output stream so it never interleaves with a concurrent sendEvent(common.dto.ServerEvent).
      Parameters:
      resp - the response to write
      Throws:
      IOException - if the underlying stream fails — the caller (the request-handling loop) drops the session on failure

    • sendEvent

      public void sendEvent(ServerEvent ev) throws IOException
      Pushes an unsolicited event from the server to this subscribed client. Uses the same writeLock as sendResponse(common.dto.ServerResponse) because both write to the same ObjectOutputStream; interleaved writes would produce garbled, undeserializable bytes.
      Parameters:
      ev - the event to push
      Throws:
      IOException - if the underlying stream fails — the caller (the subscription publish loop) catches this and continues fan-out to the other subscribers

    • subscribe

      public void subscribe(SubscriptionKey key)
      Records that this client now wants change events for key. Idempotent — re-subscribing the same key is a no-op.
      Parameters:
      key - the resource subscribed to

    • unsubscribe

      public void unsubscribe(SubscriptionKey key)
      Removes a previous subscription. No-op if the key was not subscribed.
      Parameters:
      key - the resource to stop watching

    • subscriptions

      public Set<SubscriptionKey> subscriptions()
      Read-only snapshot of every subscription this session currently holds. Used by SubscriptionRegistry.unregisterAll to detach the session from every per-key bucket on disconnect.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable view of the live subscription set

    • getLoggedInActorId

      public Long getLoggedInActorId()
      Returns the id of the actor authenticated on this connection, or null if no one is logged in.
      Returns:
      the id of the actor authenticated on this connection, or null if no one is logged in

    • getLoggedInKind

      public String getLoggedInKind()
      Returns the single-login lock kind held by this connection ("USER"/"VISITOR"), or null if logged out.
      Returns:
      the single-login lock kind held by this connection ("USER"/"VISITOR"), or null if logged out

    • setLoggedIn

      public void setLoggedIn(Long actorId, String kind)
      Records (on login) or clears (on logout) who is authenticated on this connection and which single-login lock kind they hold.
      Parameters:
      actorId - the authenticated actor's id, or null to clear on logout
      kind - the lock kind "USER"/"VISITOR", or null to clear

    • close

      public void close()
      Closes the streams and the socket. IOException is swallowed so a partially-broken connection still tears down quietly. The actual "client disconnected" log line is emitted by OrderServer as [conn -] so it can carry the unsubscribe count alongside.

    • remoteAddressString

      public String remoteAddressString()
      Returns string form of the peer's socket address (or "(unknown)" if the address is no longer available) for use in log lines.
      Returns:
      string form of the peer's socket address (or "(unknown)" if the address is no longer available) for use in log lines
